questions.q: The primary function of controlling is to ensure that the set goals are achieved in a timely and efficient manner.
The controlling process involves comparing the actual performance with the standard performance.
questions.answer: The controlling process involves comparing the actual performance with the standard performance to identify the deviations. This helps in taking corrective actions to achieve the set goals.

questions.q: The controlling process is also known as feedback process.
questions.answer: The controlling process is also known as feedback process.

questions.q: The controlling process involves determining the deviations between the actual and standard performance.
questions.answer: The controlling process involves determining the deviations between the actual and standard performance. This helps in identifying the areas where corrective actions are required.

questions.q: Which of the following is not a step of controlling process?
The controlling process involves taking corrective actions to achieve the set goals.
The controlling process is the most important function of management.
The controlling process involves comparing the actual performance with the budgeted performance.
questions.answer: The controlling process involves comparing the actual performance with the budgeted performance to identify the deviations. This helps in taking corrective actions to achieve the set goals.

questions.q: The controlling process is a continuous process.
The controlling process involves determining the causes of deviations.
questions.answer: The controlling process involves determining the causes of deviations. This helps in taking corrective actions to achieve the set goals.
